notes: With-skill runs consistently produce all 6 structural elements (unknowns, first proof, scoped checkpoint, file output, review point, risk analysis)
Without-skill runs default to comprehensive end-to-end project plans - technically detailed but missing the checkpoint planning methodology
The 'file was written' assertion is non-discriminating (passes for both configurations)
Token/time overhead for with-skill is ~30% higher, which is expected given the interactive template structure
The baseline plans are not bad - they contain useful technical detail - but they plan everything at once rather than surfacing unknowns and focusing on the next checkpoint
